items.title: Banking, finance and consultancy firms evacuate staff in Gulf States
items.description: Several major international businesses closed or evacuated offices in Gulf countries after Iran threatened US- and Israel-linked economic targets and centres in the Middle East. US finance group Citi and British consultancy Deloitte evacuated offices in Dubai's financial centre, while PwC, another British consultancy, closed offices in Saudi Arabia, Qatar, the UAE and Kuwait. HSBC has closed all branches in Qatar until further notice.

items.title: Bangladesh Parliament reconvenes after uprising, polls
items.description: The government of Prime Minister Tarique Rahman, leader of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party, took over after February 12 elections

items.title: Cruise ship too fast, too wide before hitting reef, initial report finds
items.description: An early safety report into the grounding of luxury Australian cruise ship Coral Adventurer focuses on its speed and a wide turn in confined waters before it swerved into a reef.

items.title: Humanitarian crisis in Lebanon: Displaced families face despair
items.description: A new violent escalation in Lebanon – but in reality, the war that began in 2023 never truly stopped. According to the United Nations, the ceasefire signed in November 2024 between Hezbollah and Israel has been violated more than 14,000 times by Israel. Then, in late February, Iran’s supreme leader, Ali Khamenei, was assassinated in US-Israeli bombings. In response, Hezbollah – Lebanon’s pro-Iran militia – retaliated from southern Lebanon, raising the risk of plunging the entire country into yet another full-scale war. Since then, Israeli evacuation orders have forced nearly a quarter of Lebanon’s population to flee their homes.

items.title: Middle East war live: Israel to expand operations in Lebanon, threatens to 'take territory'
items.description: Israel's military has been instructed to expand its operations ‌in Lebanon, Defence Minister Israel ‌Katz said on Thursday, after Hezbollah fired a heavy barrage of ‌rockets at Israel overnight. Katz also warned Lebanon to stop the strikes or Israel "will take territory". Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Justified or not? US military families on fears of Iran war
items.description: BBC's North America editor Sarah Smith spoke to Gold Star mum Lynn, whose son died in the Iraq War, and veteran Jeremy.

items.title: Watch: Missile strike kills eight on Beirut seafront as Israel launches wide-scale attacks on Hezbollah
items.description: In Lebanon, the Health Ministry said an Israeli strike hit Beirut’s Ramlet al-Baida seafront, killing at least eight people and wounding around 21 others. The attack comes as the Israel–Hezbollah conflict intensifies, with more than 600 people killed and around 800,000 displaced in Lebanon.

items.title: Flood victims forced to line up for hours for disaster relief payments
items.description: Katherine region residents displaced from their homes by the recent flood emergency have been forced to wait in line for hours to access disaster relief payments.

items.title: ‘We won, but don’t want to leave early,’ Trump says; U.S. have knocked out 58 Iranian mine-laying ships so far
items.description: He seemed to ​signal that the U.S. would continue ⁠the fight for now; U.S. military gave a figure of 16 mine-laying boats destroyed on Tuesday (March 10, 2026)

items.title: Police accused of overreach after 'From the river to the sea' arrests
items.description: The pro-Palestinian group that organised the Brisbane protest says police were "everywhere" and "ready to make arrests" over use of the banned phrase.

items.title: UN chief condemns killing of UNICEF worker in DR Congo strike
items.description: The Secretary-General expressed outrage over the death of UNICEF’s Karine Buisset, who was killed in a drone strike on a building housing aid workers in Goma, in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

items.title: Afghan Govt says three civilians killed by Pakistani shelling
items.description: Months of cross-border clashes have flared again since February 26, when Afghanistan launched an offensive along the frontier, in retaliation for earlier Pakistani air strikes

items.title: US launches new trade probes that could lead to fresh tariffs
items.description: The inquiries could lead to new levies on key trading partners after the Supreme Court curtailed President Donald Trump's global tariff program.

items.title: Honda warns of $16bn hit on its pivot away from EVs
items.description: Japan's Honda expects to book extra costs of almost $16 billion as it recalibrates its electric vehicle strategy. It blamed US policy changes and tariffs in part, as well as reduced competitiveness in Asia.

items.title: Epstein used modelling agent to recruit girls, Brazilian women tell BBC
items.description: Modelling agent used businesses to recruit girls and arrange US visas to visit Jeffrey Epstein, Brazilian women tell BBC.

items.title: Germany news: Lufthansa pilots stage another strike
items.description: A two-day strike by Lufthansa pilots is expected to lead to many cancellations. The German chancellor is visiting Norway, a key energy supplier to his country. DW has more on events in and concerning Germany.

items.title: 'Stealth' Pokemon hit boosts Nintendo Switch 2 momentum sentiment
Djokovic makes early Indian Wells exit after upset loss
items.description: World number three Novak Djokovic falls to defending champion Jack Draper in a pulsating fourth-round match at the prestigious Indian Wells tournament.

items.title: Residents of this Beirut neighbourhood felt safe. Then Israel attacked it.
items.description: Residents of a Beirut neighbourhood tell Al Jazeera they felt safe until the Israeli attack on Wednesday morning.

items.title: Germany, others partially release oil reserves amid Iran war
items.description: Germany, Austria, Japan, Spain and France have all indicated plans to release some of their oil reserves, a day after the International Energy Agency was debating the pros and cons. The Iran war has driven prices up.

items.description: Jack Draper produces a superb fightback to beat Novak Djokovic and reach the Indian Wells fourth round - a result he believes could be "a real big moment" in his season.

items.title: US war on Iran: 'An American war in desperate search of a strategy', expert says
items.description: FRANCE 24’s François Picard is joined by Dr. Rouzbeh Parsi, Adjunct Senior Lecturer at Lund University in Sweden. According to Parsi, the current political situation in Iran should be approached with caution, as too much attention is being paid to the potential rise of Mojtaba Khamenei. The Islamic Republic is not a system built around a single individual, especially during a time of war. Decision-making power lies with institutions such as the Revolutionary Guards and the wider security establishment.

items.title: War in the Middle East: New waves of missiles hit Israel
items.description: Lebanon's Hezbollah said it launched missiles at an Israeli military intelligence base in the suburbs of Tel Aviv early on Thursday, the Iran-backed group's latest claim in a major operation against Israel it announced hours earlier. Iran's Revolutionary Guards said earlier that they had carried out a joint missile operation with ally Hezbollah against targets in Israel. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.

items.title: Matildas stars Raso, Catley out of Asian Cup quarterfinal against North Korea
items.description: Hayley Raso and Steph Catley will miss the Women's Asian Cup quarterfinal, as the team prepares for the unknown quantities that North Korea may bring.

items.title: Hong Kong authorities raid two brokers and a hedge fund in crackdown on insider trading
Iran police chief says anti-government protesters treated as 'enemies'
items.description: The warning comes after the government cracked down on anti-government protests in January, sparked a month before over economic grievances in the sanctions-hit country.

items.title: War in the Middle East: Iran attacks Gulf energy targets
items.description: Iran launched a new wave of attacks against Gulf energy targets on Thursday. The renewed drone and missile attacks against Iran's Gulf neighbours and Israel followed a warning from Tehran that it could wage a prolonged war that would "destroy" the world economy. FRANCE 24's Rochelle Ferguson Bouyahi tells us more about Iran's strategy.

items.title: Could the US military have struck a primary school by mistake?
items.description: PRESS REVIEW – Thursday, March 12: Multiple leaks to the American press by Pentagon officials point the finger at faulty US data that listed an Iranian primary school as a military target, leading to a Tomahawk strike that killed more than 170 people. In other news, UK papers react to the “Mandelson Papers”. And finally, The Guardian reports on an intrepid young fox with an American dream.

items.description: After years of drought, pastoralists in WA are desperate for fodder for their cattle. A charity has a supply in storage for them — but no money for fuel to transport it.

items.description: In order to avoid shortages as the war in the Middle East rages on, Nigeria can rely upon billionaire Aliko Dangote’s refinery in Lagos, which is capable of processing some 650,000 barrels of crude oil per day.

items.description: African football's governing body CAF says it made the decision to postpone it's top women's tournament to ensure "the best possible conditions." It's not the first time the tournament has been moved at short notice.

items.description: Iran has effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz as the U.S. and Israel continue airstrikes in the country, triggering what's being described as the "largest oil disruption in history."

items.title: There is only one person’ who can decide to end the war on Iran
items.description: Israeli journalist and writer, Gideon Levy, says the decision to end the Iran war rests only with US President Trump.

items.title: Champions League: PSG pull away late to beat Chelsea (5-2)

Scoring three goals in the last quarter of an hour, PSG seem to have restored the confidence they displayed last season. They have five days to rest before travelling to London for the second leg.

items.title: U.S. investigates India and China for ‘unfair trade practices’ as Trump tries to replace tariffs blocked by Supreme Court

items.title: Bumble shares rally on earnings beat, optimism around AI‑led app revamp
Mojtaba Khamenei as Iran's new Supreme Leader signals continuity to international community
items.description: Amid the political uncertainty surrounding Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's new Supreme Leader, Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Dr Anahita Motazed Rad, Visiting Senior Fellow in the Department of International Relations at the London School of Economics. The Islamic Republic is navigating a moment of acute pressure, both internally and externally, and that the elevation of Mojtaba Khamenei serves an important political function, explains Dr Motazed Rad. His position signals continuity within the regime’s institutional and ideological framework at a time of war. For her, the key issue is not only Mojtaba Khamenei’s personal condition but also how Iran’s political establishment manages perceptions of stability. By maintaining him as the formal leader, the regime sends a message to both domestic elites and the international community that institutional cohesion remains intact, even as multiple power centers continue to operate within the system.

items.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: Brent crude oil briefly tops $100 a barrel as Iran attacks on shipping worsen supply concerns
items.description: Iranian attacks against commercial ships around the Strait of Hormuz and Iraq's port of Basra has escalated a campaign of squeezing the oil-rich Gulf region as global energy concerns mount

items.title: U.S. will permanently close consulate in Peshawar, Pakistan, its closest mission to Afghanistan
items.description: The department notified Congress this week of its intent to close the consulate and said it would save $7.5 million per year, while not adversely affecting its ability to advance U.S. national interests in Pakistan

items.title: Google names London office 'Platform 37' in a nod to railway neighbour, AI 'Go' match
'EU needs a bit of a revolution: There is no European strategy or foreign policy'
items.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Sophie in 't Veld, former Dutch MEP and member of Volt Europa. She argues that the EU faces a profound structural crisis in its capacity to act on the global stage. What appears as policy debate is, in practice, an internal academic discussion with little consequence for international affairs. In moments of crisis, the EU is not shaping events but reacting to them, often in response to the initiatives of other powers. Ms. Veld warns that Europe has become largely irrelevant in global geopolitics.

items.title: Francis Ngannou to make MMA return in Los Angeles on May 16
items.description: Cameroonian MMA star Francis Ngannou is set to return to action on 16 May in Los Angeles, where he will face Brazilian fighter Philipe Lins, organisers announced on Monday.

items.description: We discussed both promising projects that can ‌contribute to the restoration of Russian-American relations, as ​well as the current crisis situation in global energy markets, he said

items.title: Train connecting China and North Korea arrives in Pyongyang after 6-year hiatus

China is North Korea's largest trading partner and a vital source of diplomatic, economic and political support for the isolated nuclear state.

items.description: Russia has been targeting Ukraine's railways in an effort to disrupt the country's economy and supply lines. How is Russia conducting the attacks? And can the strikes be stopped?

items.description: Poland's economy is now a European star performer. A new trend suggests further momentum, with more and more Polish firms acquiring foreign companies, including in Germany.

The evacuations at the heart of the Middle East's financial hub come as the Gulf but especially the UAE has borne the brunt of Iran's attacks in response to US-Israeli strikes.

Oil prices shot more than 9% higher as supply concerns worsened with Iranian attacks on commercial shipping around the Strait of Hormuz. The latest attacks marked an escalation in Iran’s campaign aimed at generating enough global economic pain to pressure the United States and Israel to end the war that started 12 days ago.

items.title: Mugabe's son appears in South Africa court over shooting
items.description: Following the shooting of a gardener at his home, the youngest son of former Zimbabwean ruler Robert Mugabe has appeared in a South African court

items.title: Iran signals 2026 World Cup boycott after US strikes
items.description: Iran's possible withdrawal would test FIFA's ability to keep the World Cup insulated from geopolitics. With Iran signaling it may boycott the event, the field for the sport's biggest tournament now looks less settled.

items.title: Bowen: Trump has called for an Iran uprising but the lessons from Iraq in 1991 loom large
items.description: The US president might learn that starting wars is much easier than ending them, writes the BBC's international editor.

items.title: Trial into Diego Maradona’s death given start date: April 14
items.description: Trial for late footballer’s 2020 death rescheduled for mid-April after collapse of first trial. Leer más

items.title: EU chief: Phasing out nuclear power was 'strategic mistake'
items.description: Ursula von der Leyen said the European Union was "completely dependent" on expensive and volatile fossil fuel imports. She has announced new ambitions to harmonize nuclear regulations and roll out smaller reactors.

items.title: Why a historic release of oil reserves may not tame surging prices
items.description: Oil prices continue to soar despite the International Energy Agency's plans to release 400m barrels into the market.

items.title: Oil price surges as Iran steps up attacks on ships in the Persian Gulf
items.description: Markets seesawed on Day 13 of the war in the Middle East, as two oil tankers were struck by projectiles near Iraq's southern ports and attacks between Israel and Hezbollah intensified.

items.title: Spain withdraws ambassador to Israel as rift intensifies
items.description: Spain's government has formally dismissed its ambassador to Israel, six months after recalling her in a dispute prompted by the war in Gaza. Its Tel Aviv embassy will be led by a charge d'affaires until further notice.

items.title: Haiti: Human Rights Watch sounds alarm on drone strikes
items.description: According to Human Rights Watch, the Haitian government is using its security forces and contractors to launch drone strikes on armed gangs -- strikes that are also impacting civilians.

items.title: West Asia conflict: G7 leaders agree to examine option of escorting ships to navigate freely in Gulf
items.description: The statement ‌was issued after French President Emmanuel Macron had convened a call ‌with G7 leaders ​to discuss the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran ⁠and its impact on rising energy prices

items.title: Trump urges Latin America to use military against cartels
items.description: Trump tells a group of Latin American leaders at security summit in Miami that Washington "will not tolerate the lawlessness in our hemisphere any longer." Leer más

items.title: Prosecutor in Argentina pushes AMIA bombing case toward trial in absentia
items.description: Thirty years on, prosecutor seeks trial in AMIA bombing; Ten suspects and newly identified Iranian official targeted in renewed push for justice. Leer más

items.title: Swiss police probe if deadly bus fire was 'deliberate act'
items.description: A bus fire in the Swiss town of Kerzers killed at least six people and injured several others. Investigators say early indications point to a possible deliberate act.

items.title: News24 | Container ship hit as Iran chokes Strait of Hormuz amid war with US, Israel
items.description: A container ship was hit off the coast of the United Arab Emirates by an unidentified projectile, a British maritime security agency reported, as Iran retaliates across the Gulf in response to US-Israeli strikes.

items.title: Anthropic seeks appeals court stay of Pentagon supply-chain risk designation
Live: 'Absolute madness': Broncos remain winless after Eels win bizarre NRL clash
items.description: The Broncos' defence is labelled "pathetic" as they surrender a 20-6 lead, hit back in the second half but eventually lose 40-32 on the back of future teammate Jonah Pezet's game-sealing try. Follow live.

items.title: News24 | 16 ships hit in Strait of Hormuz, too risky to provide escorts, says US Navy
items.description: Explosive-laden Iranian boats appear to have attacked two fuel tankers in Iraqi waters, setting them ablaze and killing one crew member, after projectiles struck four vessels in Gulf waters, said port, maritime security and risk firms.

items.title: Nissan, Uber, Wayve unveil robotaxi tie-up
Nomura hires FX traders in Asia on bet high volatility to drive demand
A future which works
items.description: Milei’s determination to open up one of the world’s most closed economies is moving at vastly superior speed to the painstaking parliamentary passage of the structural reforms local companies need to compete. Leer más

items.title: China’s key NPC meeting comes to a close as lower growth target set
items.description: The National People's Congress signals firm stance against corruption as China's 15th five-year plan is approved.

items.title: Billionaire accused of 'arrogance' as hundreds of mine workers laid off
items.description: Up to 238 workers at Tahmoor Colliery in south-west Sydney are being made redundant as liquidators attempt to sell the coal mine, following months of financial turmoil under Sanjeev Gupta's GFG Alliance.

items.title: BioNTech founders step down to start new venture
items.description: Ugur Sahin and Özlem Türeci were instrumental in developing the first COVID-19 vaccine to be approved in the West. Now they say they "are ready to become pioneers once again."

items.title: Six vessels attacked amid reports of Iranian drone boats, sea mines
items.description: Iraq reportedly suspends oil terminal operations after two tankers hit by explosive-laden boats in Iraqi waters.

items.title: Japanese sake brewery to make foray into Indian market
items.description: Takahashi Shouten, based in the city of Yame in Fukuoka Prefecture, launched a subsidiary in India in February.

items.title: Turkey: Heated trial of Istanbul Mayor Imamoglu opens
items.description: The contentious trial of Istanbul's deposed mayor, once the main rival to President Erdogan, got off to a rocky start. The opposition CHP party calls the corruption investigation against it politically motivated.

items.title: Ghana grants citizenship to members of African diaspora
items.description: New Ghanaian citizens took the oath of allegiance on Monday at a swearing in ceremony in Accra.

items.title: Trump the liberator goes to war
items.description: Though few would disagree that a “diplomatic solution” to the Iranian problem would be far better than one reached at gunpoint, efforts to persuade the leaders of the regime that it would be in their interest to call a genuine halt to their nuclear programme were always bound to fail. Leer más

items.title: News24 | Muslim group blames Trump immigration crackdown for record high US Islamophobia
items.description: Record ‌high Islamophobia in the US in 2025 was driven ⁠in part by President Donald Trump’s crackdown against pro-Palestinian protests and immigration, a Muslim ⁠advocacy group said.

items.title: IEA announces record oil stockpile release over Iran supply disruptions
items.description: The International Energy ​Agency on Wednesday agreed to release a record 400 million barrels of oil from strategic stockpiles to combat a spike in global crude prices since the start of the US-Israeli war with Iran, with the US contributing the bulk of the supply.

items.title: Video shows moment point-blank gunshot misses Indian politician
items.description: Senior Kashmir leader Farooq Abdullah escaped unhurt after the incident and the suspect is in custody.

items.title: The Iran war and the uncertain future of the expats
items.description: The Gulf will continue to matter for India, but the old certainty of the “Gulf dream” has been shaken

items.title: Trump-LatAm conservative summit to discuss democracy
items.description: Donald Trump will host a dozen transcontinental allies this weeekend, many of whom he has backed or helped put into office. Leer más

items.title: Milei signs labour reform introducing new rules on contracts, strikes and severance
items.description: New legislation reshapes employment contracts, severance rules, strike regulations and collective bargaining while creating a new employer-funded labour assistance system. Leer más

items.title: Iran’s sea mines raise fears of Strait of Hormuz oil disruption
items.description: Tehran may seek to choke off oil traffic through the vital Strait of Hormuz following US and Israeli strikes on Iran, raising fears it could deploy sea mines, officials say. President Donald Trump said US forces struck 28 Iranian mine-laying vessels as concerns grow over threats to the strategic shipping lane.

items.title: Man jailed for killing Perth music promoter in fight
items.description: Jardyn Rigo is sentenced to more than six years in jail for killing music promoter Andrew Scally in a street fight.

items.title: News24 | Israel strike kills 7 in Lebanon, Hezbollah announces major new operation
items.description: Lebanon said an Israeli strike on central Beirut’s seafront killed at least seven people, another attack in the heart of the capital as Iran-backed Hezbollah launched more missiles at Israel.

items.title: Polish opposition's PM candidate looks to attract far right
items.description: Poland's populist Law and Justice party is losing voters to the far right. In a bid to reverse that trend, Catholic hard-liner Przemyslaw Czarnek has been tapped as lead candidate for the 2027 parliamentary election.

items.title: Is free-to-view football coming back to Argentina?
items.description: Longtime football executive from Córdoba, Emeterio Farías, says that AFA will announce the return of “cost-free” television for supporters imminently – potentially including the top flight, though not for some time. Leer más

items.title: Watch: US strikes Iranian aircraft, claims Tehran is losing air power
Milei reactivates Santa Cruz dams, consolidating strategic rapprochement with China
items.description: Argentina’s government has confirmed that work on the hydroelectric complex on the Santa Cruz river – which has been paralysed since Javier Milei came to power – has resumed. The project, integrally financed by Chinese banks and implemented by the Gezhouba company, returns to the agenda amid an uptick in the relations between Buenos Aires and Beijing. Leer más

items.title: AI lab Anthropic sues to block Pentagon blacklisting
items.description: Tech start-up Anthropic has refused to meet the Pentagon's demands for unrestricted military use of AI, setting up a legal showdown with the Trump administration.

items.title: News24 | Iran's new supreme leader injured but 'safe', officials say
items.description: Iran’s new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, who has not spoken or been seen in public since his appointment at the weekend, is injured but “safe”, officials said on Wednesday.

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: Film: Dancing through resistance in Belleville
items.description: Under the theme “Resistance and Revolt: The Power of Images,” the 2026 Geneva International Film Festival and Forum on Human Rights is spotlighting the documentary Belleville Will Always See Us Dance.

items.title: India joins 30 UN Member States to express deep alarm at escalation of hostilities in Lebanon
items.description: Indian peacekeepers with a long standing commitment to the UNIFIL continue to execute their mandate and provide help to locals even during these challenging times, India’s Permanent Representative to the UN Ambassador Parvathaneni Harish said

items.title: France walks ‘fine line’ as US-Israel war on Iran escalates
items.description: President Macron has criticised the onslaught and questioned the war's legality but ultimately blamed Iran.

items.title: The Pussycat Dolls confirm reunion: 'We're celebrating where we're at as women'
items.description: Nicole Scherzinger, Ashley Roberts and Kimberly Wyatt announce a new single and world tour.

items.title: South Africa deploys troops in Johannesburg to tackle organized crime
items.description: Soldiers were deployed on the streets of South Africa’s biggest city on Wednesday after the president announced plans last month to use the army to help police fight gang violence and illegal mining.

items.title: Argentina to see ‘trade improvement’ from Middle East war, Milei says
items.description: War in the Middle East could generate “an improvement” in Argentina’s oil and agricultural exports, says Milei. Leer más

items.title: ADF member granted bail on charge of assault, robbery of fellow soldier
items.description: A Darwin-based soldier accused of assaulting and robbing a fellow service person has been bailed to travel home to NSW.
